creator: cy@FreeBSD.org
text:
Wow that's a large offset and jitter.

It should look more like this:

     remote           refid      st t when poll reach   delay   offset  jitter
==============================================================================
 0.freebsd.pool. .POOL.          16 p    -   64    0    0.000    0.000   0.001
+s206-75-147-25. 192.168.10.254   2 u    3  128  377   31.505    0.469   0.650
*ntp1.torix.ca   .PTP0.           1 u   65   64  377   63.519    0.246   1.018
+nms.switch.ca   206.108.0.131    2 u   63  128  377   28.519   -0.945   0.667
-bhs2.vxnd.net   206.108.0.133    2 u  129  128  377   82.168    7.876   1.091
-draco.spiderspa 213.251.128.249  2 u    7  256  377   83.962    5.603   0.434


or this:

     remote           refid      st t when poll reach   delay   offset  jitter
==============================================================================
 0.freebsd.pool. .POOL.          16 p    -   64    0    0.000    0.000   0.000
*cwfw            206.108.0.131    2 u   44   64  377    0.295   -0.384   0.282
+cwsys           10.1.1.254       3 s   49   64  376    0.379   -0.699   0.197
+bob             10.1.1.254       3 s   10   64  377    0.441   -0.507   0.249
-nms.switch.ca   206.108.0.131    2 u   40   64  377   28.827   -1.526   0.339
-162.159.200.1   10.28.8.252      3 u   40   64  377   11.723    2.663   0.846

LAN or upstream network connectivity looks to be a problem.

ping -c 10 each of your upstream ntp servers (though I suspect they block ICMP ECHO) but worth testing anyway. You could try a traceroute instead.
